[2025-06-16 03:16 | Shaena Robertson] Anthony, Good morning. Development has completed the work on the rerouting. Here are some items that came out of the development and what we will need to look at when doing in test. File given only has CR not CR+LF at end of lines so compensated. If they change will affect the processing. After looking at the file I used columns 1, 3 and 4 for account number, book, and sequence. I did not use the location in the file as that rarely gives great matching. The account numbers in the file have had their leading zeroes stripped, so I had to do a numerical equivalence to match customer records (ie convert 000031 in DB to a literal 31 before comparing to file). This probably will not be a problem unless they have varying account number lengths and have accounts that have the same value with different leading zeroes (ex 00031 and 00031 are two different account in our system but numerically it is the same so a single value in the file may match more than one customer in system). MANY of the records in the file are for customers who are not at a location in the DB we have here. I am not sure how old the data is here, so we will use your database copy live to test and then test out this process with up to date data. I return a list of accounts not found, a list of accounts that do not have a service location, and a list of accounts with service locations without the group. After it updates the book/seq on the account, it checks the books affected and if any duplicates, will resequence them. We are ready to deliver this in test. Do you have new file to try out? If not you will need to let us know when you have a file and we can schedule to copy live to test and run on the test environment. Please let us know at your earliest convenience.

To void unwanted payments in PowerManager Utility Billing EZ, follow these steps: Go to Utility Billing > Processing > Adjustments Enter the correct Posting Date for the adjusting entries (this date will be used for both Accounts Receivable and General Ledger transactions) Click Adjust Select the customer whose payment you want to void Go to the Payments tab In the Payment Selection Grid, click on the row containing the payment you wish to void (the selected payment will be highlighted) You can identify payments by their PayDate, RefNr, Amount, PayMethod, BatchNr, and Comment Click the Void button at the bottom right (only click ONCE) The selected payment will appear in the Adjustments section at the bottom of the screen Click Save to complete the void process Important Notes: Only click the Void button once to avoid the "Records already exist" error message Voiding a payment generates reversing entries that cancel out the original amount If Accounts Receivable is interfaced with General Ledger, reversing entries will also be posted to General Ledger This process should be used when a check does not clear the bank or when a payment is posted to an incorrect customer

One other question that I have is that when I go into utility billing and pull up the aged accounts receivable report the first account on the list shows that he is carrying a credit in recycling but when I go into customer inquiry it shows that his credit was brought over from our old system (simple city) under electric so I just don't understand why on the aged accounts receivable report it is showing the credit to recycling. [2026-03-10 04:00 | Mitch Mullins] Typically new rates are added for each type of service to be written off. We do this so we can assign a bad debt or a uncollectable type of expense account. This is preferred instead of reducing the sales revenue account. We would want to review the customer that is showing the credit in the recycling type of service. Not really enough information to respond to this question.

[2026-03-10 05:00 | Mitch Mullins] Typically new rates are added for each type of service to be written off. We do this so we can assign a bad debt or a uncollectable type of expense account. [2026-03-10 08:19 | Sreya Johny] Krystal, Yes, you will need to create new rates specifically for each type of service (electric, water, sewer, and recycling) that you want to write off. This is the recommended approach because: It allows you to assign appropriate bad debt or uncollectable expense accounts in your General Ledger It keeps your write-offs separate from regular service revenue accounts It provides better financial tracking of written-off amounts by service type Creating these specialized write-off rates ensures proper accounting treatment instead of simply reducing your sales revenue accounts. The discrepancy between where the credit appears (recycling in the aged accounts receivable report vs. electric in customer inquiry) could be due to how the credits were transferred when imported from your old system (Simple City). PowerManager has a "Transfer Credits" function that can move credits between service types. It's possible that during the data migration, this credit was inadvertently transferred to recycling while the history record still shows it originated in electric. To investigate this further: Check the customer's transaction history to see if there was a credit transfer transaction Look at the payment history to verify which service type originally received the credit If you'd like us to look at this specific customer account in more detail, please provide a screenshot of the customer history screen and attach the AR report.

PM - Payroll.full_thread:
[2026-03-02 04:08 | Amy Wegener] I have a new garnishment for payroll. I went in and set up the vendor and then in the garnishment, I edited it and added the vendor, but it didn't process with my payroll.

[2026-03-03 03:13 | Susan Thomas] Hi Amy, Please try the following and let us know how it goes. Proposed Solution This issue is likely caused by one of two common problems with garnishment processing: 1) The garnishment needs to be properly set up with an AP vendor assigned to it, and 2) The liability GL accounts for the garnishment deduction need to be set up in Payroll Maintenance before finalizing the pay period. The garnishment is actually processed during the payroll finalization process, not during the initial payroll calculation. Steps to complete Verify that the garnishment deduction has an AP vendor properly assigned to it in the employee's deduction setup Go to Payroll > Maintenance > Setup and select the Liability Lookup tab Ensure that GL accounts have been assigned to the new garnishment deduction If the pay period has already been calculated but not finalized, go to Payroll > Processing > Update Click the Distribution button to refigure the distribution with the updated GL accounts Click the Finalize button to complete the payroll process and generate the AP invoice for the garnishment The garnishment will now appear as a payable in Accounts Payable that can be processed and paid
